items.title: CMS issues Medicaid 'community engagement' rule: 80 hours/month requirement, states must implement by Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: CMS issued an interim final rule implementing a new Medicaid community engagement requirement for certain adults: generally 80 hours per month of qualifying work or similar activity as a condition of eligibility. CMS says states must generally implement the requirement no later than 2027-01-01, and states may begin earlier.

items.title: SNAP retailers face new stocking standards: USDA says broad compliance starts Nov. 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA finalized updated staple food stocking standards for retailers that accept SNAP, including a new framework for meeting staple-food variety requirements. USDA’s retailer notice says that starting 2026-11-04, most SNAP-authorized retailers (other than specialty stores like butchers and farm stands) must comply or risk losing the ability to redeem SNAP benefits.

items.title: USDA list shows multiple states implementing SNAP purchase restriction waivers in 2026
items.summary: USDA posted state SNAP food restriction waivers that restrict purchases such as soda and candy, with several 2026 target dates. Examples include Arkansas (target 2026-07-01) and Colorado (target 2026-10-30) as well as earlier 2026 targets like Idaho (2026-02-15) and Florida (2026-04-20), depending on the waiver.

items.title: USDA announces up to $4 million for a FY2026 SNAP Healthy Fluid Milk Incentive project
items.summary: USDA says that for fiscal year 2026 it will provide up to $4 million for a competitive cooperative agreement to carry out a Healthy Fluid Milk Incentive project. The agency’s resources page describes this as a SNAP-related grant opportunity for a government or nonprofit entity.

items.title: HUD reminds applicants: NOFO applications due 11:59:59 PM ET on May 26, 2026 (late submissions ineligible)
items.summary: HUD’s NOFO FAQ states that applications were due by 11:59:59 PM Eastern Time on 2026-05-26, and applications received after the deadline are considered late and ineligible. HUD also notes the application period was 60 days (instead of 30 days) and that only completed applications received by Grants.gov on or before the deadline count as timely.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: USDA seeks comments through July 27, 2026 on SNAP standard utility allowance (SUA) information collection
items.summary: USDA published a notice about SNAP state agency standard utility allowance (SUA) methodology submissions, stating that written comments must be received on or before 2026-07-27. The notice references a 2024 final rule requiring states to submit SUA methodologies for approval at least every five years.

items.title: DC SNAP: new ABAWD forms released June 1, 2026; fixed 36-month clock runs June 2026–May 2029
items.summary: DC’s Department of Human Services says that starting 2026-06-01, SNAP customers ages 18–64 who are not exempt are subject to ABAWD work requirements (80 hours averaged monthly) and a 3-month limit in a 36-month period. DC says it uses a fixed 36-month clock for all customers that starts 2026-06-01 and ends 2029-05-31, and it released three new ABAWD-related forms on June 1, 2026.

items.title: KFF tracker: federal Medicaid work requirement starts Jan. 1, 2027; some states plan earlier enforcement
items.summary: KFF summarizes the 2025 reconciliation law’s Medicaid work requirement for ACA expansion adults starting 2027-01-01 and tracks state implementation decisions. KFF reports Nebraska began enforcing early via a state plan amendment on 2026-05-01, with Montana planning 2026-07-01 and Iowa planning 2026-12-01; Arkansas plans a July 2026 launch but says it won’t disenroll people prior to 2027-01-01.
